title: Hot composting without a bin
subtitle: Building a free-standing hot heap and turning it to finished compost in 6-8 weeks
excerpt: Hot composting builds a heap of at least 1 m³ all at once, combines the correct carbon-to-nitrogen balance, and reaches 55-65°C within 48 hours. Turning every 3-4 days moves the cooler outer material to the hot centre. The result is finished compost in 6-8 weeks rather than 12-18 months. It requires a critical mass of material, a thermometer, and sustained effort.
sourceNotes: Berkeley 18-day hot composting method (UC Cooperative Extension). Garden Organic hot composting guidance. WRAP compost-at-home guidance on thermophilic composting.

content.text: works differently from cold composting: the heap is built all at once from pre-assembled material rather than filled gradually. The mass and correct
allow the temperature to climb into the thermophilic zone (55-65°C), where decomposition is fast and weed seeds are killed. Each turning cycle brings cooler outer material to the hot centre.

You need at least 1 m³ (1,000 litres) of mixed material. Roughly equal volumes of browns (cardboard, straw, wood chip) and greens (grass clippings, food waste, fresh plant material). Shred or tear everything to pieces no larger than 50 mm. Collect material over a week and stockpile rather than adding it as generated.

Build the heap in 150 mm alternating layers of browns and greens. Water each layer as you go, the whole heap should feel like a wrung-out sponge. Aim for a cube shape to maximise volume-to-surface-area ratio. A cylindrical shape or a cube 1 m × 1 m × 1 m is sufficient.

Insert the compost thermometer 30 cm into the heap after 24 hours. A well-built heap reaches 55°C within 48 hours. If it does not: check moisture (add water if dry), check volume (too small to self-insulate), or check nitrogen content (add more greens).
Step 4, turn the heap every 3-4 days.

Move the outer cooler material to the centre and the hot centre to the outside. After turning, the temperature drops briefly then rises again. After 5-6 turns (3-4 weeks), the heap stops reheating after turning, decomposition is slowing. Continue turning less frequently for another 2-3 weeks until temperature stabilises below 40°C and the material smells earthy.

items.fix: Check volume is at least 1 m³. Add nitrogen-rich material (grass, food scraps). Water throughout.
items.cause: Insufficient mass, incorrect C:N ratio, or too dry

items.fix: Normal at the end of the process. Add a small amount of fresh green material to extend the hot phase if needed, then allow to mature.
items.cause: Carbon or nitrogen has been consumed, the heap is transitioning to cold composting
items.symptom: Heap heats once then won't reheat after turning

items.fix: Add more torn cardboard or wood chip. Turn to aerate.
items.symptom: Ammonia smell during the hot phase
